title: video-tile/v1
description: Embedded video player primitive for SOCIII canvases. Universal across workers per the bees-in-hive composition pattern + Trump Rule visual-floor discipline. Video is the most attention-magnetic primitive available; supported as a first-class canvas element.
inheritsFrom: CODEX S52.43 (Platform RAAS Invariants — Reagan Rule + EH-01)

source.description: Origin of the video. Determines embed strategy + Reagan Rule treatment.

sourceUrl.description: Canonical URL to the video. For platform_archive: a SOCIII storage URL. For user_supplied: the upload reference. For news_url: the article page hosting the video (not the embed).

embedUrl.description: Pre-rendered embed URL (e.g., YouTube oEmbed iframe URL). Optional if displayMode = thumbnail_with_overlay and the player only loads on user click.

attribution.description: Human-readable attribution displayed on the tile. Examples: 'KHON2 News', 'YouTube — @PinedaleWY', 'Maui County Council Archive', 'User upload'.

verified.description: Per Reagan Rule (CODEX S52.43 §5): true ONLY if source has been cross-referenced against an authoritative source OR is from platform_archive. user_supplied + news_url + direct_upload default to false until verified. UI displays an 'unverified' badge if false.

verificationMethod.description: How the verified flag was determined. Surfaced in tooltip + receipt.

retrievedAt.description: When the video reference was retrieved or uploaded. Stamped on every PLAT-008 receipt that references this video.

retrievedHash.description: SHA-256 of the source URL + retrievedAt timestamp + (for platform_archive) the storage object hash. Pinned for Deposition Rule audit chain.

displayMode.description: How the tile renders. thumbnail_with_overlay (default): static thumbnail with play overlay, expands to player on click. inline_player: embedded player visible in canvas. hero_video: full-width video above the canvas tabs (for marquee uses like marketing or webinars).

thumbnailUrl.description: Static thumbnail image URL. For YouTube/Vimeo: auto-fetched via oEmbed. For direct_upload: extracted at upload time. For news_url: scraped from og:image meta tag or supplied manually.

durationSeconds.description: Video length in seconds. Surfaced in tile chrome (e.g., '4:32').

transcript.description: Optional transcript for accessibility + searchability + worker reasoning.

topicTags.description: Human-readable topic tags surfaced in the tile chrome. Examples: 'post-disaster rebuild', 'rezone hearing', 'drone tour', 'investor briefing'.

relatedEntityRef.description: Optional reference to the entity (parcel / matter / case / etc.) this video is contextually attached to. Used by Vault DTC logbook + cross-worker recall.

auditAnchored.description: Whether the video reference is recorded in PLAT-008 audit anchor at the moment of canvas summoning. Default true; false only for ephemeral previews.

examples.attribution: Maui County Council — Zoning Workshop 2026 (official archive)

notes.reagan_rule_application: user_supplied + news_url + direct_upload default to verified: false. The worker may surface the video and reason FROM it, but the UI displays an 'unverified' badge until a verification method is recorded. Worker outputs that reference user-supplied videos must label conclusions as 'based on user-supplied video, not independently verified.'
notes.britney_rule_application: If an AI suggests a video URL, it MUST be retrieved via an external resolver (e.g., YouTube Data API, news article fetch) and the retrievedHash recorded. A model-recalled URL is never a valid sourceUrl. The PLAT-EH-01 validator check applies to videos same as to legal citations.
notes.deposition_rule_application: Every video summoned into a canvas is recorded in PLAT-008 audit anchor with the retrievedHash. Three years later, a forensic auditor can verify the video reference was authentic at the time of analysis. If the source URL goes dead, the audit chain survives — the hash binds the reference to its time.
notes.accepts_contract_application: Any worker that declares 'accepts: ["video-tile/v1"]' in its catalog entry gains the embed capability. Universal across workers — Site Recon embeds drone aerials, Land Use embeds council hearings, Marketing embeds Kling-generated walkthroughs, Title Abstract embeds property tour videos.
